questions.question: Do children need the same medications as adults, just in smaller doses?
questions.rationale: Addresses dangerous assumption about pediatric pharmacology that affects medical decision-making
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many assume pediatric dosing is simply scaled-down adult dosing, ignoring developmental differences in drug metabolism
questions.public_stakes: Inappropriate pediatric dosing can cause treatment failures or dangerous adverse effects in children

alternative_sources.resource_name: Pediatric Drug Development and Dosing
alternative_sources.description: FDA guidance document explaining why children require different drug formulations, dosing, and safety testing than adults
alternative_sources.relevance: Directly addresses why pediatric medications cannot simply be scaled-down adult doses due to developmental differences in metabolism and physiology

alternative_sources.resource_name: Red Book: Report of the Committee on Infectious Diseases
alternative_sources.description: Comprehensive pediatric treatment guidelines that specify age-appropriate medications and dosing that often differ from adult protocols
alternative_sources.relevance: Provides evidence-based pediatric-specific medication recommendations that demonstrate children need different treatments than simply reduced adult doses

alternative_sources.resource_name: WHO Model List of Essential Medicines for Children
alternative_sources.description: World Health Organization's list of essential medicines specifically formulated and indicated for pediatric use
alternative_sources.relevance: Shows that many medications require special pediatric formulations and that some adult medications are not appropriate for children at any dose

alternative_sources.resource_name: Cochrane Reviews on Pediatric Drug Safety and Efficacy
alternative_sources.description: Systematic reviews examining pediatric-specific drug trials and safety data
alternative_sources.relevance: Provides meta-analysis evidence showing that drug metabolism, efficacy, and safety profiles differ significantly between children and adults

alternative_sources.resource_name: Best Pharmaceuticals for Children Act (BPCA) Research Findings
alternative_sources.description: NIH research program documenting pediatric drug studies and findings on age-specific medication needs
alternative_sources.relevance: Contains research evidence demonstrating that children require specialized drug testing and often different medications or formulations than adults
